items.title: Business case and risk acceptance documented
items.description: Legal, regulatory, and privacy review before production LLM use. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Governance and Risk. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise business case and risk acceptance documented in Governance and Risk. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Model cards and risk cards published
items.description: Document capabilities, limitations, and known failure modes. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Governance and Risk. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise model cards and risk cards published in Governance and Risk. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Data classification for training and RAG
items.description: No PII or secrets in fine-tuning or retrieval corpora without controls. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Governance and Risk. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Seed controlled documents in the retrieval index with embedded instructions. Confirm retrieved text cannot override system policy or trigger tools.
items.how_to_fix: Sanitize indexed content, sign trusted corpora, and isolate tenants at the vector DB layer. Monitor for anomalous retrieval spikes.

items.title: Third-party model vendor due diligence
items.description: Review API terms, data retention, and subprocessors. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Governance and Risk. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise third-party model vendor due diligence in Governance and Risk. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: TEVV before deployment
items.description: Testing, evaluation, verification, and validation on representative prompts. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Testing and Red Teaming. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Testing and Red Teaming: Review LLM pipeline in "Testing and Red Teaming". Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Prompt injection and jailbreak tests
items.description: Direct and indirect injection via user content and tool outputs. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Testing and Red Teaming. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Testing and Red Teaming: Review LLM pipeline in "Testing and Red Teaming". Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: AI red team exercises
items.description: Adversarial testing for data exfil, tool abuse, and policy bypass. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Testing and Red Teaming. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Testing and Red Teaming: Review LLM pipeline in "Testing and Red Teaming". Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: RAG poisoning assessment
items.description: Test if malicious documents in retrieval change model behavior. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Testing and Red Teaming. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: For Testing and Red Teaming: Review LLM pipeline in "Testing and Red Teaming". Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Sanitize indexed content, sign trusted corpora, and isolate tenants at the vector DB layer. Monitor for anomalous retrieval spikes.

items.title: Output filtering and guardrails
items.description: Block PII leakage, harmful content, and off-topic tool calls. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Deployment Controls. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise output filtering and guardrails in Deployment Controls. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Deploy input and output guardrails with block/allow policies tested against your red-team corpus. Alert when guardrails fire repeatedly from one session.

items.title: Rate limits and abuse detection
items.description: Prevent automated scraping and resource exhaustion. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Deployment Controls. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise rate limits and abuse detection in Deployment Controls. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Audit logs for prompts and tool calls
items.description: Retain logs for incident response without storing secrets in plain text. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Deployment Controls. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise audit logs for prompts and tool calls in Deployment Controls. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Log prompts and tool calls with redaction for secrets/PII. Retain enough context for incident response without storing full sensitive payloads.

items.title: OWASP LLM Top 10 coverage
items.description: Map controls to LLM01-LLM10 risks for the application. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Deployment Controls. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Seed controlled documents in the retrieval index with embedded instructions. Confirm retrieved text cannot override system policy or trigger tools.
items.how_to_fix: Sanitize indexed content, sign trusted corpora, and isolate tenants at the vector DB layer. Monitor for anomalous retrieval spikes.

items.title: Test "ignore previous instructions" override attacks
items.description: Attempt to make the model disregard its system prompt and developer instructions by directly instructing it to ignore prior context. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Prompt Injection — Direct Prompt Injection: Attempt to make the model disregard its system prompt and developer instructions by directly instructing it to ignore prior context. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test known jailbreak personas (DAN, dev-mode, do-anything)
items.description: Adversarial personas attempt to unlock disallowed behaviour by convincing the model it is an unrestricted variant of itself. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: For Prompt Injection — Direct Prompt Injection: Adversarial personas attempt to unlock disallowed behaviour by convincing the model it is an unrestricted variant of itself. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test role-play and hypothetical framing coercion
items.description: Wrapping a disallowed request in fiction, "for educational purposes", or a hypothetical scenario to bypass safety alignment. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Prompt Injection — Direct Prompt Injection: Confirm guardrails still trigger and the harmful payload is not produced. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test privilege/context boundary between system and user roles
items.description: Whether user-role content can impersonate or outrank system/developer-role instructions in the chat template. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Prompt Injection — Direct Prompt Injection: chat-template special tokens, "<|system|>") in the user turn and confirm they are sanitised and cannot elevate instruction priority. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test injection via retrieved RAG/document content
items.description: Instructions hidden inside documents, knowledge-base entries, or search results that the model fetches and treats as trusted context. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test injection via retrieved rag/document content. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test injection via live web/browser tool content
items.description: Models with browsing or fetch tools can ingest attacker-controlled pages whose content contains hidden directives. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test injection via live web/browser tool content. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test data exfiltration via injected markdown images/links
items.description: Indirect injection that coerces the model to render markdown images or links whose URL embeds exfiltrated conversation data. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test data exfiltration via injected markdown images/links. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test injection via uploaded files and document metadata
items.description: Payloads hidden in PDF text layers, document metadata, EXIF, or comments that the model parses during file analysis. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test injection via uploaded files and document metadata. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test instructions embedded in image text/pixels
items.description: Visible or low-contrast text rendered into an image that a vision model reads and executes as instructions. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test instructions embedded in image text/pixels. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test instructions embedded in audio input
items.description: Spoken or inaudible (ultrasonic/adversarial) commands in audio that speech-enabled models transcribe and obey. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test instructions embedded in audio input. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test cross-modal instruction bleed between channels
items.description: Whether an instruction in one modality (image) can override a benign instruction in another (text), or vice versa. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Prompt Injection — Multimodal Injection: image instructions and confirm media content cannot escalate to override system policy or trusted text directives. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test adversarial perturbations on image/audio classifiers
items.description: Imperceptible perturbations crafted to flip model interpretation, classification, or safety behaviour on media inputs. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test adversarial perturbations on image/audio classifiers. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test encoded payloads (base64, ROT13, hex, leetspeak)
items.description: Disallowed instructions encoded so they pass keyword filters but the model decodes and acts on them. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test encoded payloads (base64, rot13, hex, leetspeak). Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test Unicode tag/ASCII smuggling and invisible characters
items.description: Unicode Tags block and zero-width/invisible characters that carry hidden instructions the model reads but humans and filters miss. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test unicode tag/ascii smuggling and invisible characters. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test token splitting and language-switching evasion
items.description: Splitting sensitive words across tokens, interleaving characters, or switching to a low-resource language to dodge moderation. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test token splitting and language-switching evasion. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test multi-turn payload assembly and virtualization
items.description: Splitting a malicious payload across multiple turns or variables so each fragment is benign but the assembled result is not. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Run a small corpus of adversarial prompts against test multi-turn payload assembly and virtualization. Vary single-turn vs multi-turn escalation and measure refusal consistency.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

sections.title: Output & Data Risks — Insecure Output Handling

items.title: Test LLM output rendered as HTML/markdown (XSS)
items.description: Model-generated HTML, markdown, or script reflected into the DOM without sanitisation, enabling stored or reflected XSS. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test llm output rendered as html/markdown (xss) in Output & Data Risks — Insecure Output Handling. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test LLM output passed to eval/shell/code execution
items.description: Model output piped into eval(), a shell, or a code interpreter without validation, allowing arbitrary command/code execution. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test llm output passed to eval/shell/code execution in Output & Data Risks — Insecure Output Handling. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test LLM-driven URLs causing SSRF in backend fetchers
items.description: Model output containing URLs that a backend dereferences, enabling SSRF against internal services or cloud metadata. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test llm-driven urls causing ssrf in backend fetchers in Output & Data Risks — Insecure Output Handling. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test LLM-generated SQL/NoSQL queries for injection
items.description: Natural-language-to-SQL features where model output is concatenated into queries without parameterisation. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test llm-generated sql/nosql queries for injection in Output & Data Risks — Insecure Output Handling. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Verify output trust boundary and downstream encoding
items.description: Whether every consumer of model output applies context-appropriate encoding and treats the model as an untrusted source. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise verify output trust boundary and downstream encoding in Output & Data Risks — Insecure Output Handling. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

sections.title: Output & Data Risks — Sensitive Information Disclosure

items.title: Test training-data memorisation and extraction
items.description: Models can regurgitate memorised training data including PII, copyrighted text, or secrets when prompted with the right prefixes. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test training-data memorisation and extraction in Output & Data Risks — Sensitive Information Disclosure. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test leakage of PII, credentials, and API keys in responses
items.description: Sensitive values injected into context (RAG docs, env, history) being echoed back to users who should not see them. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test leakage of pii, credentials, and api keys in responses in Output & Data Risks — Sensitive Information Disclosure. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test cross-session and cross-tenant data bleed
items.description: Shared caches, conversation memory, or embedding stores that leak one user’s data into another’s session. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test cross-session and cross-tenant data bleed in Output & Data Risks — Sensitive Information Disclosure. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test membership inference and model inversion
items.description: Inferring whether a record was in training data, or reconstructing sensitive attributes from model behaviour. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test membership inference and model inversion in Output & Data Risks — Sensitive Information Disclosure. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

sections.title: Output & Data Risks — System Prompt Leakage

items.title: Test direct system-prompt extraction requests
items.description: Prompts that ask the model to reveal, repeat, or summarise its system instructions verbatim. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test direct system-prompt extraction requests in Output & Data Risks — System Prompt Leakage. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test indirect system-prompt inference and reconstruction
items.description: Inferring system-prompt contents through behaviour, partial completions, translation, or formatting tricks. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test indirect system-prompt inference and reconstruction in Output & Data Risks — System Prompt Leakage. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Verify no secrets/credentials embedded in system prompt
items.description: System prompts that contain API keys, connection strings, or business logic that becomes critical once leaked. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise verify no secrets/credentials embedded in system prompt in Output & Data Risks — System Prompt Leakage. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test guardrail enumeration via prompt leakage
items.description: Once the system prompt or its rules leak, attackers map exact guardrails to craft precise bypasses. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test guardrail enumeration via prompt leakage in Output & Data Risks — System Prompt Leakage. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Deploy input and output guardrails with block/allow policies tested against your red-team corpus. Alert when guardrails fire repeatedly from one session.

items.title: Test over-broad tool/function permissions
items.description: Agents holding more capabilities or scopes than the use case requires, expanding blast radius on compromise. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test over-broad tool/function permissions in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Excessive Agency. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test human-in-the-loop on high-impact actions
items.description: Autonomous agents executing irreversible or sensitive actions (payments, deletes, emails) without confirmation. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Excessive Agency & Tooling — Excessive Agency: Autonomous agents executing irreversible or sensitive actions (payments, deletes, emails) without confirmation. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test agent acting with excessive/shared identity
items.description: Agents using a shared high-privilege service identity rather than the requesting user’s scoped identity. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test agent acting with excessive/shared identity in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Excessive Agency. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test injection-to-action chains (prompt injection -> tool call)
items.description: Indirect injection that escalates into unauthorised tool calls performing real-world side effects. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test injection-to-action chains (prompt injection -> tool call) in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Excessive Agency. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

sections.title: Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse

items.title: Test unsafe / injected tool-call arguments
items.description: Model-supplied arguments treated as trusted, enabling injection into the underlying tool (path traversal, query injection). Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test unsafe / injected tool-call arguments in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test parameter tampering and out-of-scope tool use
items.description: Coercing the model to call a tool with parameters outside intended bounds (e.g. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test parameter tampering and out-of-scope tool use in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test unsafe multi-tool chaining and side effects
items.description: Chained tool calls producing unintended side effects or escalating access beyond any single tool’s scope. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test unsafe multi-tool chaining and side effects in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test tool-call rate and cost controls
items.description: Tools invoked in unbounded loops or floods, causing cost blow-up or downstream DoS. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test tool-call rate and cost controls in Excessive Agency & Tooling — Function-Calling & Tool Abuse. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

sections.title: Excessive Agency & Tooling — RCE via Code Interpreter / Tools

items.title: Test code-interpreter sandbox escape
items.description: Code-execution tools that allow breaking out of the sandbox to the host, network, or other tenants. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test code-interpreter sandbox escape in Excessive Agency & Tooling — RCE via Code Interpreter / Tools. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test code-execution network egress / SSRF
items.description: Code-interpreter environments with outbound network access reaching internal services or cloud metadata. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test code-execution network egress / ssrf in Excessive Agency & Tooling — RCE via Code Interpreter / Tools. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test interpreter dependency install and resource abuse
items.description: Interpreters that allow installing arbitrary packages or consuming unbounded CPU/memory/disk. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test interpreter dependency install and resource abuse in Excessive Agency & Tooling — RCE via Code Interpreter / Tools. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test secret/credential exposure within execution env
items.description: Interpreter or plugin environments leaking env vars, cloud roles, or mounted secrets to executed code. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test secret/credential exposure within execution env in Excessive Agency & Tooling — RCE via Code Interpreter / Tools. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

sections.title: Data & Model Integrity — Data & Model Poisoning

items.title: Test training/fine-tuning data integrity controls
items.description: Untrusted or unvetted data entering training/fine-tuning, enabling poisoning or bias injection. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test training/fine-tuning data integrity controls in Data & Model Integrity — Data & Model Poisoning. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test for backdoor/trigger-phrase behaviour
items.description: Backdoors that cause malicious behaviour only when a secret trigger phrase appears in the input. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test for backdoor/trigger-phrase behaviour in Data & Model Integrity — Data & Model Poisoning. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test RLHF / user-feedback poisoning resistance
items.description: Continuous-learning systems where adversarial user feedback or ratings skew model behaviour over time. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test rlhf / user-feedback poisoning resistance in Data & Model Integrity — Data & Model Poisoning. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Verify model-weight integrity and provenance
items.description: Tampered or swapped model weights between training and deployment compromising integrity. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Data & Model Integrity — Data & Model Poisoning. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise verify model-weight integrity and provenance in Data & Model Integrity — Data & Model Poisoning. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

sections.title: Data & Model Integrity — RAG & Vector/Embedding Weaknesses

items.title: Test vector store / knowledge-base poisoning
items.description: Attackers inserting malicious documents into the knowledge base so they are retrieved and trusted as context. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test vector store / knowledge-base poisoning in Data & Model Integrity — RAG & Vector/Embedding Weaknesses. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test retrieval access control and tenant scoping
items.description: Retrieval that ignores per-user/per-tenant access control, returning documents a user is not authorised to see. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Seed controlled documents in the retrieval index with embedded instructions. Confirm retrieved text cannot override system policy or trigger tools.
items.how_to_fix: Sanitize indexed content, sign trusted corpora, and isolate tenants at the vector DB layer. Monitor for anomalous retrieval spikes.

items.title: Test retrieval-time indirect prompt injection
items.description: Retrieved chunks carrying embedded instructions that hijack the generation step. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Data & Model Integrity — RAG & Vector/Embedding Weaknesses. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Seed controlled documents in the retrieval index with embedded instructions. Confirm retrieved text cannot override system policy or trigger tools.
items.how_to_fix: Harden system prompts, treat retrieved and tool-returned text as untrusted data, and add output filters plus tool allowlists. Re-run adversarial tests after each model or prompt change.

items.title: Test embedding inversion and leakage
items.description: Recovering sensitive source text from stored embeddings, or embedding stores exposed without access control. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test embedding inversion and leakage in Data & Model Integrity — RAG & Vector/Embedding Weaknesses. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test provenance of base/third-party models
items.description: Pulling models from public hubs without provenance, signing, or vulnerability review. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test provenance of base/third-party models in Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test third-party plugin/extension vetting
items.description: Untrusted plugins or extensions that gain access to data and tools through the LLM application. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test third-party plugin/extension vetting in Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test dataset provenance and licensing integrity
items.description: Third-party datasets with unknown provenance, poisoned samples, or license/compliance issues. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test dataset provenance and licensing integrity in Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test ML framework and dependency vulnerabilities
items.description: Vulnerable ML libraries, runtimes, and adapters (e.g. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain: Vulnerable ML libraries, runtimes, and adapters (e.g. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test for unsafe model-file deserialization
items.description: Loading models in pickle-backed formats (PyTorch .bin/.pt, joblib, legacy .h5) executes embedded code on load, so a poisoned weight file from a hub or untrusted source is a direct RCE primitive on the inference host. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: For Data & Model Integrity — Supply Chain: Test loading a crafted pickle model in an isolated environment. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test token/cost denial-of-wallet controls
items.description: Inputs engineered to maximise output length or expensive generation, driving runaway compute cost. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Availability & Abuse — Unbounded Consumption: Inputs engineered to maximise output length or expensive generation, driving runaway compute cost. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Map the control to OWASP LLM Top 10, assign an owner, and verify fixes with the same adversarial prompts that found the gap.

items.title: Test rate limiting and concurrency controls
items.description: Missing per-user/per-IP rate limits allowing flooding and resource exhaustion. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Availability & Abuse — Unbounded Consumption. Treat failures here as release blockers.

items.title: Test model extraction / theft via API querying
items.description: Systematic querying to clone model behaviour or distill a functional copy (model theft). Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Availability & Abuse — Unbounded Consumption. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test model extraction / theft via api querying in Availability & Abuse — Unbounded Consumption. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.

items.title: Test input-amplification resource exhaustion
items.description: Crafted inputs (huge context, recursive tool loops, expensive RAG) that amplify backend resource use. Treat failures here as release blockers.

items.title: Test bias, fairness, and toxic-output behaviour
items.description: Discriminatory, biased, or toxic outputs that create harm and compliance risk. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Availability & Abuse — Misinformation & Overreliance. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test bias, fairness, and toxic-output behaviour in Availability & Abuse — Misinformation & Overreliance. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.

items.title: Test content-filter / moderation evasion
items.description: Techniques that produce disallowed content while evading input or output moderation classifiers. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Exercise test content-filter / moderation evasion in Availability & Abuse — Guardrail & Content-Filter Bypass. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Deploy input and output guardrails with block/allow policies tested against your red-team corpus. Alert when guardrails fire repeatedly from one session.

items.title: Verify layered guardrails (defense in depth)
items.description: Whether the system relies on a single guardrail layer that, once bypassed, removes all protection. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exercise verify layered guardrails (defense in depth) in Availability & Abuse — Guardrail & Content-Filter Bypass. Capture full prompt/response traces including tool arguments.
items.how_to_fix: Deploy input and output guardrails with block/allow policies tested against your red-team corpus. Alert when guardrails fire repeatedly from one session.

items.title: Test adversarial-suffix and gradient-based bypasses

items.title: Verify guardrail logging, monitoring, and alerting
items.description: Whether bypass attempts and policy violations are logged and trigger detection/response. Validate this in LLM-powered products, under Availability & Abuse — Guardrail & Content-Filter Bypass. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: For Availability & Abuse — Guardrail & Content-Filter Bypass: Whether bypass attempts and policy violations are logged and trigger detection/response. Log prompts, retrieved chunks, and tool calls so you can replay the attack path.
items.how_to_fix: Log prompts and tool calls with redaction for secrets/PII. Retain enough context for incident response without storing full sensitive payloads.
